text: You suddenly realize that you don't have enough money to purchase what you want.
speaker: narrator

text: Blustering, you say to Shema:  "Your prices are too high for me!"
speaker: hero
say: Your prices are too high for me!

text: As your face turns scarlet with embarrassment, Shema smiles and says:
speaker: narrator

text: "We are sorry not to be able to show more generosity, but we, too, are victims of hard and evil times."
speaker: shema

text: You must pay the innkeeper for a room.
speaker: narrator

text: This looks like an alpine inn, complete with dishes on the wall and heavy beams on the ceiling.  It is pleasantly warm, and the aroma of strange spices fills the air.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a small, cat-like creature known as a Katta.  You've heard that Kattas are common in the southern deserts.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a rather attractive female Katta wearing her native costume.
speaker: narrator

text: "Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema."

text: It's too early to go to bed; you don't feel tired enough to sleep.

text: Because your silver is too low, you must find other accomodations.  Perhaps you can find work somewhere.
speaker: narrator

text: "The tavern is located in the northwest corner of our town."
speaker: shameen

text: "The Guild Hall is just down the street from here."
speaker: shameen

text: "Zara's magic shop is next door to my Inn.  You can't miss it."
speaker: shameen

text: "I have run this inn for three years now, with Shema."
speaker: shameen

text: "We had thought to return to Shapeir, my homeland, with Abdulla Doo, but Alas, Fate has decreed otherwise."
speaker: shameen

text: "Very nice room.  Very soft beds, finest in town.  Only five silvers a night."
speaker: shameen

text: I have little knowledge of these things.  Perhaps if you shop around..."
speaker: shameen

text: "Poor Abdulla sits.  He tries to forget his loss in food and drink. I think he will not be sorry if you join him at his table."
speaker: shameen

text: "My friend, Abdulla, is recovering from his robbery last week.  He should be here around suppertime."
speaker: shameen

text: "The world is filled with many kinds of creatures, both good and bad."
speaker: shameen

text: "Although in this valley I now have friends, I miss my homeland terribly."
speaker: shameen

text: "My apologies, Wanderer, but for such drink you must go to the tavern at the other end of town."
speaker: shameen

text: "Alas, the brigands stole all of Abdulla's wealth and mine own when they robbed his caravan.  Now I am forced to stay in this wet, cold land."
speaker: shameen

text: "I hoped to return to my homeland with the profits Abdulla, Shema, and I would make from the caravan.  Now, I fear, we will remain here unless someone defeats the brigands and returns our stolen treasures."
speaker: shameen

text: "Hot spice.  Hot!  I hope you like!"
speaker: shameen

text: "We live far to the south in the desert lands.  Someday, we may yet return there."
speaker: shameen

text: "Work of an adventurous sort is available.  You must go to the Hall of the Adventurer's Guild."
speaker: shameen

text: "I know little about them except that they must be wealthy now."
speaker: shameen

text: "I am Shameen, mate of Shema, innkeeper to the town of Spielburg."
speaker: shameen

text: "Shema is my mate and the light of my life. If you sit down, she will be happy to serve you."
speaker: shameen

text: We Katta are a race of desert dwellers, wanderers of the endless sands."
speaker: shameen

text: "I am but a poor unfortunate, forced to seek my fortune in this cold land, far from my homeland, Shapeir, and my people."
speaker: shameen

text: "We of the Katta know only low and simple spells."
speaker: shameen

text: "Alas, this humble innkeeper is ignorant of that about which you ask."
speaker: shameen

text: "A thousand pardons!  I am a fool and have no answer for your question."
speaker: shameen

text: "Forgive me, that I am unable to speak with knowledge on that subject."
speaker: shameen

text: Shema, mate of Shameen the innkeeper.
speaker: narrator

text: "It is my pleasure to serve you."
speaker: shema

text: "Then you shall order food or drink."
speaker: shema

text: "I am so sorry.  I do not understand."
speaker: shema

text: "Then please enjoy our table and our fire."
speaker: shema

text: "We have very good stew of newborn lamb along with honey chicken for a mere 3 silvers."
speaker: shema

text: "We have rich black coffee, freshly ground and brewed as only my people do.  Such coffee will bring you to your full alertness."
speaker: shema

text: "We also have a tea of the rarest herbs, which will quench the thirst of the desert.  Each is only one silver."
speaker: shema

text: "For that sort of beverage you must go to the tavern."
speaker: shema

text: "I am called Shema, mate of Shameen, your humble servant."
speaker: shema

text: "Shameen is of the oldest and finest ancestry of the Katta people."
speaker: shema

text: "Shapeir is a land of desert sands, flowers, fruit, and beauty.  How I wish to return."
speaker: shema

text: She merely blushes, as if embarrassed.  She appears to be very shy.

text: You just did.

text: "Please pay for your order after I bring it."
speaker: shema

text: "With great pleasure will I serve you a fine meal."

text: The food tastes surprisingly good.

text: Collection of designer plates loaned to the Hero's Inn by Mary Meister.
speaker: narrator

text: The warmth of the fire feels good on such a chilly day.  The proprietor of the inn apparently thinks so, too.  Cats always seem to gravitate to a warm hearth.
speaker: narrator

text: This table must have been made for some tall folks, perhaps Goons, Ogres, or Centaurs.
speaker: narrator

text: A door to some other room.  Perhaps the proprietor can tell you what's in there.
speaker: narrator

text: The Baron von Spielburg looks like he was once a strong man, but sorrow has aged him greatly.
speaker: narrator

text: The Baronet looks his usual haughty self, although his clothes are a little neater than the last time you saw him.
speaker: narrator

text: The cupboard contains a variety of dishware and eating utensils.
speaker: narrator

text: The von Spielburg coat of arms:  Twin Saurus guarding, Winged Chevron, on a field Azure.
speaker: narrator

text: The fireplace is cold and lifeless, the grate in front now only an ornament.
speaker: narrator

text: The guards stand stiffly at attention behind the Baron's table.
speaker: narrator

text: The long tables are covered with dusty tablecloths.
speaker: narrator

text: Through the door, you can see the hallway through which you entered, and the stairs leading down to the main entrance.
speaker: narrator

text: That would be inappropriate behavior here.
speaker: narrator

text: You realize, of course, that such actions will not only have you grabbed by guards, dragged to the dungeon, and brutally tortured, but that you probably won't be able to win this game if you perform them.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor has not been cleaned in some time.
speaker: narrator

text: The Great Hall of Spielburg Castle is not very impressive.  The room is plain, with scuffed floors and grimy plastered walls.
speaker: narrator

text: You are less than awed by Baron von Spielburg's Great Hall.  You'd think a Baron could keep his home a little cleaner.  You try to hide your disappointment.
speaker: narrator

text: "Words cannot express the gratitude I have towards you."
speaker: baron

text: "Not only have you freed the Baronet from enchantment and allowed my son to return to me, but you have renewed my hope that the curse may be lifted."
speaker: baron

text: "My son, Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, also desires to acknowledge his indebtedness."
speaker: baron

text: "Barnard."
speaker: baron

text: "We, er, that is, I, am appreciative of your efforts upon my behalf."
speaker: baronet

text: "Furthermore, I understand you have rid our valley of a dangerous Kobold Magic User in order to free the Baronet von Spielburg."
speaker: baron

text: "You risked your life to singlehandedly defeat the foul Spellcaster.  You are a true hero, and I thank you."
speaker: baron

text: "I'm pleased you managed to kill the Kobold."
speaker: baronet

text: "I sincerely hope you will continue on your valiant adventures in our land.  If anyone can rid the land of the brigands, it will be you."
speaker: baron

text: "Someday in the near future, I can envision holding a ceremony in your honor and bestowing upon you the title, 'Hero of the Land of Spielburg'."
speaker: baron

text: "It would be nice if someone could finally defeat the brigands and claim the title."
speaker: baron

text: "As you leave the castle tomorrow, you will receive the reward money I long ago posted for the safe return of my son, as a token of my thanks."
speaker: baron

text: "You will, of course, be expected to dine with us and be our guest in the castle tonight."
speaker: baron

text: "Do you have any questions?"
speaker: baron

text: "Just ask about anything you wish to know."
speaker: baron

text: "I see that it is getting late."
speaker: baron

text: "Some years back, I tried to force the Ogress Baba Yaga to leave our valley, and she put a curse upon me that I should lose everything I held dear."
speaker: baron

text: "When I lost my daughter and my son, I lost all interest in living.  My lands have suffered because I suffered."
speaker: baron

text: "Now, though, with the return of my son, I trust the prophecy may yet come true, thanks to you."
speaker: baron
baron

text: "You entered here from the East, and you released my son from the form of a bear.  I am certain you can fulfill the rest of it, and remove this dreadful curse."
speaker: baron

text: "I have attempted everything in my power to get her to go away.  I fear it will take some sort of magic to be able to counteract her evil magic."
speaker: baron

text: "I know very little of magic, much to my sorrow.  Perhaps you should talk to the wizards -- Zara in the town, or Erasmus on Zauberberg."
speaker: baron

text: "Zauberberg, the 'magic mountain', is the home of Erasmus, a kindly, but rather eccentric, wizard.  Zauberberg is directly east of here."
speaker: baron

text: "My beautiful little daughter was stolen away ten years ago by Baba Yaga's magic."
speaker: baron

text: "She has never been found, despite all the searching that has been done for her."
speaker: baron

text: "I thought she was lost forever, but you have renewed my hope that I will again have her beside me."
speaker: baron

text: "My wife has been gone for many years."
speaker: baron

text: "She died giving birth to Elsa, my daughter."
speaker: baron

text: "If I had more guards, I would wipe the brigands out myself, now that I have my son at my side."
speaker: baron

text: "It will take some time before this castle and land have recovered from my sorrow, so I still think it will take a Hero to destroy the brigands."
speaker: baron

text: "I know they have some sort of fortress at the far south of the valley.  I have heard rumors they have set up an ambush for all that approach."
speaker: baron

text: "It is also rumored that there is another way into their fortress, but I have no further knowledge about it."
speaker: baron

text: "Yorick was my court jester, a man of infinite jest.  He went off to find my daughter, but alas, poor Yorick never returned."
speaker: baron

text: "I have offered substantial rewards for the return of my son and my daughter, as well as for capturing or killing the leader and warlock of the brigands."
speaker: baron

text: "There have always been brigands around, but until their new leader and the warlock showed up a few years ago, they were not much of a problem."
speaker: baron

text: "The leader seems to be a brilliant strategist who manages each raid with little loss.  Once their leader is gone, I am sure the brigands can be easily defeated."
speaker: baron

text: "As if this valley hasn't been plagued with enough evil magic users!  From what I have heard, his magic isn't powerful, but he uses it effectively."
speaker: baron

text: "This castle has been in the family for two centuries now.  It was a gift from King Siegfried the Third."
speaker: baron

text: "I rode off one morning with my men in search of monsters to fight.  I got separated from my men when I came across the entrance to the cave."
speaker: baronet

text: "As I entered the cave, the Kobold ordered ME to get out."
speaker: baronet

text: "I informed it that I was Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, and heir to the lands and all within.  I ordered it to bow before me."
speaker: baronet

text: "Then it cast a spell upon me, and I remember very little after that."
speaker: baronet

text: "I would prefer not to talk about that."
speaker: baronet

text: As you can plainly see, Barnard is back to his old self, thanks to you.
speaker: narrator

text: "Now that my son is back, I have much to do.  Kindly restrict your questions to matters of import."
speaker: baron

text: "That is of no importance to your quest, and time is short."
speaker: baron

text: "A servant is awaiting you outside to show you to your quarters.  We will see you at supper later."
speaker: baron

text: Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall. In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.

text: That's what happens sometimes when you go out on a limb.

text:
"Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness,
Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury,
Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!"
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn,
Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame,
Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!"
speaker: brauggi

text: (It looks like it will take a lot of fruit to fill up THOSE fists!)
speaker: narrator

text: From the blue color of his skin and his huge height, you can tell this is a Frost Giant.
speaker: narrator

text: The Giant (who does not seem pleased at your casting a spell in his presence) radiates a magical aura.  The strongest magic centers on the glowing gem at his belt.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't offer what you don't have.
speaker: narrator

text: The Giant looks at you in disgust.  Apparently, you did not offer what he desires.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Near now is North, for the winter has wandered,
Bringing this brave one to barter so bold.
I journey from Jotunheim, home of the giants,
Source of all snowstorms, the sender of sleet."
speaker: brauggi
brauggi

text:
"Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor,
Apples or oranges, peaches or pears."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow,
That brings joy to Giants and madness to men."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field,
A jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Bored I become when the banter so blindly
Speaks of the subjects I care not about."
speaker: brauggi

text: "Annoy me not over matters so minor."
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Waste not my wits, for I weary of words;
My axe it is asking for action and more."
speaker: brauggi

text: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.
speaker: narrator

text: The snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.

text: The gem glows like an aurora in the night sky.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see a gem here.
speaker: narrator

text: Brauggi has departed on the long journey to his homeland.
speaker: narrator

text: Brauggi has become impatient with your questions and gone elsewhere.  You will have to return another time.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter,
Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi,
Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me."
speaker: brauggi

text:
Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores,
Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered;
The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.
speaker: brauggi

text:
"Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless,
My axe it is edged as is the ice.
This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum,
The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood."
speaker: brauggi

text: Next time, pick on someone your own size.
speaker: narrator

text:
"Courage you carry, but skill it is scanty;
Face not this foe before fiercer you fight."
speaker: brauggi

text: No sooner do you draw your weapon then the guards have their weapons out and quickly disarm you.  It seems that they practice with the Baron's Weapons Master.  They promptly drag you into the castle and down into the dungeon, which is not a very likely spot for the attainment of hero status.

text: There is an absence of the usual litter associated with town streets. This hamlet is kept very clean.
speaker: narrator

text: The evening is clear and the stars are bright. Dark clouds pass over the moon.
speaker: narrator

text: The sky is a piercing blue with scudding white clouds.
speaker: narrator

text: The town wall is made of the same rock as the surrounding mountains.It appears to be very sturdy.
speaker: narrator

text: It keeps the occupants of the building dry when it rains.
speaker: narrator

text: If you look past the town wall, you can see the mountains through which you journeyed to arrive here.
speaker: narrator

text: You curl up on the hard ground and try to get some rest.
speaker: narrator

text: This is the barracks area.
speaker: narrator

text: The castle is northeast of here
speaker: narrator

text: The building's appearance is deceptive; it evidently extends well into the castle wall.  From your present vantage point, the barracks looks like it could house some sixty guards.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the castle and the guards.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the main courtyard of the castle and the stables beyond.
speaker: narrator

text: Along the wall to the southeast is the gatehouse.  A line of bushes partially obscure a defensive pit along the wall.
speaker: narrator

text: You can see the barracks and a man sitting in front of them.
speaker: narrator

text: The guard would object.
speaker: narrator

text: You barely get to sleep when the guard on night patrol kicks you out.
speaker: narrator

text: The guard looks like he is either very tired or very lazy.
speaker: narrator

text: You're not close enough.  He's hard of hearing.
speaker: narrator

text: "Hay is for horses."
speaker: guard

text: "If you want to fight, go talk to the Weapons Master."
speaker: guard

text: As you approach the guard with your weapon, he jumps up and opens the barracks door as he yells for help.  Four guards playing cards inside the barracks quickly grab their weapons and overwhelm you.  As you are dragged into the castle and down into the dungeon, you realize that was probably not quite the right thing to do.
speaker: narrator

text: "I'm here!  What do you want?"
speaker: guard

text: "What?"
speaker: guard

text: Go ahead.
speaker: narrator

text: "I'm not here to answer your stupid questions.  Go talk to Karl the gatekeeper.  He talks to anyone about almost anything."
speaker: guard

text: Just a fence.
speaker: narrator

text: These are the mountains that ring the valley of Spielburg.
speaker: narrator

text: This is the forest that surrounds the castle.
speaker: narrator

text: The guards patrol from here.
speaker: narrator

text: "Go away.  This is just the barracks for us guards."
speaker: guard

text: The air is crisp here and you can see your breath as you walk. There is a path leading up to a dark mountain, atop which is precariously perched a purple house.
speaker: narrator

text: The path seems to be carved out of the rock.  It forms a steep trail up to the house on the mountain.
speaker: narrator

text: All of the mountains you can see are covered with snow except for the one to which the path leads.
speaker: narrator

text: The drifts of snow look several feet high in some places.
speaker: narrator

text: Looking north, you see the strange house nestled on its craggy peak.  It is hard to see much at this distance.  All you can tell is that the house is very large, very purple, and very strange.
speaker: narrator

text: To the east is the forest.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the surrounding mountains.
speaker: narrator

text: The clouds skirt the mountain edges.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a rocky path through the snow.
speaker: narrator

text: There is nothing here to throw it at.
speaker: narrator

text: You detect a strange, magical aura in this place.
speaker: narrator

text: There's nothing here to dazzle.
speaker: narrator

text: The magical aura around this place prevents you from casting a flame dart.
speaker: narrator

text: A sign appears.  It reads:
speaker: narrator

text: "Willkommen auf Zauberberg!"
speaker: narrator

text: "Welcome to Magic Mountain!"
speaker: narrator

text: "Bienvenu a Mont Magie!
speaker: narrator

text: Another sign appears.  It reads:
speaker: narrator

text: "Trespassers will be toad!"
speaker: narrator

text: "Now go home!"
speaker: narrator

text: "Proceed at your own risk!"
speaker: narrator

text: "BE STILL!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Critics!  We can't all be gourmands, I suppose."
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
Now, Oh-Soon-To-Be-Supper...
I don't suppose you have a name?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: You try your best to croak out your name, or at least let the witch know that you do indeed have one.
speaker: narrator

text: "So you're the one who's trying to be a hero around here.  The only good hero's a dead hero, I always say!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "But I do have a need for a brave fool.  Are you brave?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: You make little froggy sounds, trying your best to indicate your agreement.
speaker: narrator

text: "Wellll..."
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "If you're willing to do a small little teensy favor for me, I might reconsider having you for supper."
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"I need the root of a mandrake plant
that grows in the graveyard.  Will you be
a sweet and fetch me some?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Anything but Frog Legs Fricassee!", you think.   You croak your agreement to the task.
speaker: hero

text: "And I had my mouth watering for frog.  Oh well!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"Hear what I say
And hear me right:
Mandrake must be pulled
At precisely Midnight!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"This I tell you
And this I say:
Return with the root
Ere the break of next day."
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"Hear what I say
And know I don't lie:
Bring back the root
Or else you will die!!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "You, Shoo!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "E's me friend, 'e and Fenrus.  Powerful wizard 'e is."
speaker: hermit

text: "I 'ere 'is 'ouse is sumthin' to see up north."
speaker: hermit

text: "I don't make it out much, and 'e's pretty busy, sos I don't see 'em too often.  But when we do all get together, you can be sure we'll 'ave fun."
speaker: hermit

text: " 'E loves to play 'is games, 'e do."
speaker: hermit

text: "Fenrus is Erasmus' familiar or is that the other way around?"
speaker: hermit

text: "Erasmus' favorite game is the Mage's Maze.  'Ave to be a magic user to play it, though.  Erasmus is awful good at it."
speaker: hermit

text: "Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times.  Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them"
speaker: hermit

text: "E's not so bad.  Got a good sense o' 'umor, 'e 'as. I get the giggles just to think of 'im.  He heh he heh he."
speaker: hermit

text: " 'E's come by at times to chat.  Borrowed the mirror wot I borrowed from Erasmus 'e did."
speaker: hermit

text: "A magic mirror of reflection, it was.  If you use it, when a nasty spell was cast at you, it was wot sent it back at the one wot cast it.  Do un to others, I sez.  Heh, he, he."
speaker: hermit

text: It's hard to be a hero when you're sitting in a jail cell.  The Sheriff apologized for your broken arm, but he did warn you (didn't he?) that Otto was only partly trained.  In the future, you'll probably be more careful when you're robbing someone.
speaker: narrator

text: When it comes to breaking in, it looks like the only thing broken is your head!  When at last you come to your senses, you find yourself in a jail cell.  By the time you get out of here, you'll be too old to be a hero.
speaker: narrator

text: Uh Oh! As you see a pillow flying toward you, you hear the Sheriff's wife say...
speaker: narrator

text: "SCREEEEEEEEEEEECH!"
speaker: sheriff-wife

text: You never dreamed a feather pillow could be so HARD!  Or was it the floor?  You also didn't realize how hard it is for a thief to be a Hero.  The Thief of Baghdad should have this kind of luck!
speaker: narrator

text: "Let's finish this game first."

text: "Come in, come in.  Good to see you again.   Get's kind o' tiresome sitten here an' thinken by meself all the time."
speaker: hermit

text: "Ello.  'Ow are you?  'Ave we met before?"
speaker: hermit

text: "I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me Farther was an 'ermit and me Murther was an 'ermit sos I come by me job rightly."
speaker: hermit

text: "Don't 'ave too many visitors.  'Ermits don't, you know.  (Part o' the job description.) I likes to see a new face, though."
